name: Deep-stack 3-bet pot AhKd2c, ~500bb eff
description: Extreme deep stack scenario: 500bb effective (eff=4910 chips, pot=180 = 18bb after 3-bet). Very high SPR (~27) means there are MANY more streets of action possible than a normal 100bb 3-bet pot. Tests the solver's handling of deep-stack dynamics: bet sizing should slope up, overbets should appear, more check-back and float lines are viable. Same board as spot_012 but the strategy must be materially different. Hero = BB, villain = BTN.

input.hero_range: TT, JJ, QQ, KK, AA, AJs, AQs, AKs, AQo, AKo, KQs, A5s, A4s, A3s, A2s
input.villain_range: QQ, KK, AA, AKs, AKo, A5s, A4s, KQs

expected_notes: Populated by differential run. The deep stack should surface overbet lines, higher bluff frequencies, and more balanced check-back ranges compared to spot_012. If our output closely matches spot_012, we have an SPR-handling bug.
